goal: Prove that OpenSearch is operationally ready for later Cortex MVP work.
preconditions: The OpenSearch service definition exists in the platform deployment repos.
Moltbox gateway lifecycle commands are available on the operator machine.
The dedicated NVMe data path and backup-HDD snapshot path are available or explicitly stubbed for the target host.

tests.title: Gateway deploy and service status
tests.objective: Prove the service deploys through the standard Moltbox lifecycle without ad hoc Docker-only steps.
preconditions: Service metadata and compose templates are in place.
procedure: Run `moltbox gateway service deploy opensearch`.
Run `moltbox gateway service status opensearch`.
Capture the reported service state for the run artifact.
expected_results: Deploy succeeds through the gateway pipeline.
Status reports the service as healthy or healthy enough for further inspection.
failure_conditions: Deploy fails through the gateway pipeline.
Status never reports a usable service state.

tests.title: Health, version, and plugin inventory
tests.objective: Prove the running service is reachable, exposes native inspection paths, and carries the required plugin or tooling set.
procedure: Query cluster health through the documented operator path.
Run `moltbox opensearch --version`.
Inspect plugin inventory through the documented operator path.
Record the exact command path used for health and plugin inspection.
expected_results: Cluster health is queryable after deploy.
Native CLI passthrough is reachable through Moltbox.
Required plugins are present: k-NN, Security, SQL, Index Management, and Performance Analyzer.
failure_conditions: Native CLI passthrough is missing or undocumented.

tests.title: Resource, mount, and security posture
tests.objective: Prove the live service reflects the intended RAM, CPU, storage, backup, and security posture.
procedure: Inspect the rendered service configuration or live container state.
Confirm the configured memory, CPU, and no-GPU posture.
Confirm the data path resolves to the dedicated NVMe target.
Confirm the snapshot repository path resolves to the intended backup mount.
Confirm the Security plugin is enabled and admin credentials are present through the documented secret path.
expected_results: Service posture matches the intended 64 GiB memory allocation and 4 CPU cores.
No GPU requirement is present.
Data and backup mounts resolve to the intended devices or mount points.
Security is enabled.
failure_conditions: Live posture drifts from the intended memory, CPU, or mount targets.
Backup path is missing or mis-mounted.
required_evidence: Rendered config or container inspection output.

tests.title: Operator CLI smoke index lifecycle
tests.objective: Prove operators can create, inspect, write to, and delete a temporary index using the documented command path and credential model.
preconditions: An operator credential and Cortex service-account path both exist.
procedure: Use the documented operator path to create a temporary `cortex-smoke-<id>` index.
Insert one sentinel document into the smoke index.
Read the sentinel document back.
Delete the original smoke index.
Record the exact command path and credential path used.
expected_results: Temporary index lifecycle works through the documented operator path.
Sentinel document is readable after write.
Command and credential paths are explicit enough for later reuse.
failure_conditions: Operator path for index lifecycle does not exist.
Temporary index cannot be created, inspected, or deleted.

tests.title: Snapshot repository and nightly policy
tests.objective: Prove backup configuration is active rather than merely documented.
procedure: Verify `path.repo` and the mounted snapshot path.
Register the filesystem snapshot repository.
Run repository verification.
Verify or create the nightly snapshot-management policy with explicit retention.
expected_results: Snapshot repository registration succeeds.
Repository verification succeeds.
A nightly policy with explicit retention exists.

tests.title: Smoke snapshot and restore drill
tests.objective: Prove the backup can actually be used to restore data.
procedure: Trigger or await one snapshot execution that includes the smoke index.
Confirm the snapshot reaches a completed success state.
Restore into a throwaway target.
Read the sentinel document from the restore target.
Clean up the restored target.
expected_results: One snapshot completes successfully.
Restore drill succeeds.
Sentinel data is readable from the restored target.

tests.title: Cortex bootstrap, aliases, and service account
tests.objective: Prove the Cortex service account can bootstrap the Cortex-owned indexes and aliases cleanly.
procedure: Authenticate with the Cortex service account.
Run the Cortex bootstrap path.
Inspect the resulting knowledge and retrieval-trace aliases and versioned indexes.
expected_results: Cortex service account authenticates successfully.
Bootstrap is idempotent and succeeds.
Required aliases resolve to versioned indexes.
failure_conditions: Bootstrap fails or is not idempotent.
Required aliases or indexes are missing.

tests.title: Sample write, governance query, provenance, and traces
tests.objective: Prove the service can store and query Cortex-shaped data in a way later epics can rely on directly.
procedure: Index a small sample knowledge set containing one in-scope lodging idea, one unrelated same-owner document, and one similar wrong-scope document.
Index one retrieval-trace sample.
Run a governance-filtered lexical query such as `places to stay while hiking`.
Fetch the winning knowledge document by ID.
Query provenance by `artifact_id`.
expected_results: In-scope knowledge document is indexed and returned.
Wrong-scope document is excluded by governance filters.
Knowledge document is readable by stable ID.
Provenance and retrieval trace are inspectable.
failure_conditions: Governance filters fail to exclude wrong-scope data.
ID, provenance, or trace lookups fail.

exit_criteria: Required evidence exists for each test case.
The service is ready to be assumed by later Cortex epics without additional explanation.
